headline: Co-Founder at Mutinex | We're Hiring!
summary:
Entrepreneur at Mutinex, with a focus on leading, growing, product and operations. Background in software engineering and digital strategy, with high proficiency in solution architecture, analytics and Python/Ruby programming languages. 

Under-30 Achiever of the Year 2020 @ Mumbrella, Media Week Next of the Best Leadership winner in 2024, numerous awards for entrepreneurship and business building. I grow businesses and build products - it's that simple.

Director at Exit Capital, with a focus on investing in and growing data / database businesses.

experiences.title: Co-Founder
experiences.description:
Co-founder. Product guy. Software guy.  

We help companies manage billions in growth investment decisions better.

The software version of Mutinex began at the start of 2022. Before that, it was a consultancy.
experiences.location: New York, New York, United States

experiences.title: Investor
experiences.description: Investor via FWBC. Invested pre-seed, company now at Seed stage recently raising $2.2m @ $8m (up from $500k @ $4m).

experiences.title: Investor
experiences.description:
Investor @ ReciMe. Occasional advisor. 

ReciMe has gone from some ~3,000 users when I invested to being one of the largest food recipe apps on the web, boasting hundreds of thousands of users.

experiences.title: Director
experiences.description:
Director at Exit Capital, boutique investment and mid-market M&A specialist.

Investments include Costmine, MB, TrueState and a range of other small positions in early stage software comapnies.

experiences.description:
Investment and guidance (software, sales, operations) for key investments and startups. 
Former include: Intierra (exited), Urban Walkabout (shut down), ASM (exited), Peazie (badly exited), NRH (exited) and Trendwise (shut down)

Occasionally looking for interesting projects

experiences.title: Strategy
experiences.description:
Strategy at VML (part of Y&R Group), working on productising VML's offering + new biz + consolidating accounts. Loads of fun with great people. 

Clients: McDonalds, Hyundai, Genea, Menulog, NRMA, Metlife, NSW Government

Pitch wins: Menulog, Genea, Metlife, Health&Co, GamblingNSW, Petbarn

Notable work:
* mymacca's app launched to number #1 in the app store in 24 hours (lowest CPI of campaigns to date)

* Strategy & digital on the Menulog 'Less Talk, More Eat' campaign, shortlisted for B&T's Ad Campaign of the Year

experiences.title: Strategy
experiences.description:
Working with great brands leading strategic direction in marketing/digital, working with startups to help with capital raises, go-to-market strategies and product development (again in the digital space). Equity partner in the business.

Grew and managed teams extensively and worked on the structure of the business.

Key achievements: 
> Driving a social & content strategy that delivered up to 50% decrease in CPA for Medibank. 
> Relaunching Medibank's social and content platforms to align to a new 'Stargate' strategy. 
> Launching a number of campaigns across content, social and digital for Medibank.
> Building the go-to-market and launching Australia's fastest growing new social network, Bombd. 
> Building the digital strategy and customer framework for KinCare.
> Developing new product in the hair loss industry, Samson & Sons. 
> Consulting extensively to the video game industry on launches and similar.

experiences.title: Intern
experiences.description:
Worked under a team focusing on experiential brand marketing. Was part of a team that brainstormed ideas, executed goals, and created high end, unconventional marketing strategies. 

Campaigns included the Pantene Secret Shampoo campaign, and a variety of pitch ideas. Assisted in the execution of campaigns by co-ordinating contractors, organizing/arranging permits, and executing ideas under my direct supervisors.

Was a very hands on experience and I loved it.

accomplishment_projects.title: Google Glass Hackthon
accomplishment_projects.description:
We built a Google Glass App (Trace) as a rapid prototype for a leading global humanitarian organisation. One of the key challenges for humanitarian organisations is the need for quick and efficient distribution of relief aid to the ones most in need. They face many challenges in making this happen due to the time it takes to relay information back to base camp.

The app allows volunteers to wear Google Glass to capture images, recordings and text(via voice recognition) and send them back to a web interface via a web service. This enables all the volunteers and base camp operators to receive data in real time which allows them to make informed decisions in order to ensure relief aid first reaches the ones most in need. Hence, the app will immensely increase relief distribution efficiency and ultimately help in saving more lives. 

The prototype was completed 24 hours starting for problem identification to implementation.

recommendations:
Keith Newton

It is refreshing to find strong commercial anchoring in such a youthful mind. It means Henry's ideas - which are so plentiful, one can only assume that there are a few Watson chips sitting in his head - demand a serious hearing. Henry lives at the forefront of the communications industry, as does his boundless passion. He is also a delight to work with. Highly recommended.

Stuart Mitchell

It’s rare to get an “agency unicorn” like Henry! He’s a very switched on and savvy operator. A strong strategic thinker, he really understood our consumers and how best to connect with them in meaningful ways. He’s also not afraid to challenge the client (which I really appreciated) and was always thinking one step ahead. If there ever was a guy who had the crystal ball into the digital future, this is the guy. 

It was an absolute pleasure to work with Henry. I really do hope I can partner with him again.

Stuart Mitchell
